US President Donald Trump posted a picture of the suspected shooter on social media.

articles.title: Trump was being shown a party trick. Then gunshots rang out

children.content: Thousands of influential players in Washington filed into a ballroom with the US president. Then shots rang out.

image.altText: a composite image of a female security service agent with gun in front of satellite image of Washington DC

articles.title: Trump was 'all set to really rip', but shooting has changed tune on media

children.content: While the latest act of violence wasn't as close as Trump's brush with death in Butler, Pennsylvania, almost two years ago, it will no doubt revive some trauma for Trump and those closest to him.

image.altText: Trump in a tuxedo

children.content: US Acting Attorney-General Todd Blanche says the shooter apprehended at the White House correspondents' dinner was likely targeting Donald Trump. Follow live.

items.title: Trump says he 'wanted to see' as gunshots rang out and 'probably' made Secret Service act more slowly

updates.title: Donald Trump speaks to 60 Minutes in US
Former US president Barack Obama commends Secret Service
White House: 'This political violence has to end'

items.title: Teacher of the month turned assassin. Who is Cole Allen?

image.altText: Man lies face down on carpet, hands bound

children.content: Details from the life of US President Donald Trump's would-be assassin paint a picture of a high achiever who took an abrupt turn towards political violence.

items.title: 'Superhero' politician shielded Kerry Kennedy at correspondents' dinner

children.content: Human rights activist Kerry Kennedy praises a "superhero" member of Congress for shielding her when an active shooter attempted to storm the annual White House Correspondents' Association dinner.

image.altText: A group of people lie on the floor of a ballroom. A blonde woman holds a man in close to her chest.

items.title: King Charles heads to US for Trump state visit after shooting incident

children.content: The British monarch and Queen Camilla are embarking on a state visit to the United States with relations between the two countries strained and following a shooting at a press gala in Washington.

image.altText: Two men holding wine glasses lean toward each other in conversation

items.title: Two dead in house fire on day family were due to move interstate

children.content: Two bodies found in a house that was extensively damaged by fire in the foothills of the NSW Blue Mountains are believed to be those of two unaccounted for children.

image.altText: Bowen Mountain heli shot

items.title: Missing 5yo girl believed to have been abducted near Alice Springs, police say

children.content: Northern Territory Police say they believe a missing five-year-old girl in Alice Springs was abducted from her home at a town camp early on Sunday morning.

image.altText: Young girl in pink dress does peace sign with fingers

items.title: Australia's fugitives found sanctuary in Istanbul. Then it all fell apart

children.content: Istanbul was once the hideout of choice for Australian crooks. Now a Sydney gangster's legal challenge has revealed how it all unravelled for the "Aussie Cartel".

gallery.altText: A graphic of a bearded man in grainy black-and-white against a red backdrop of the Turkish flag and Istanbul skyline.

thumbnail.altText: A graphic of a bearded man in grainy black-and-white against a red backdrop of the Turkish flag and Istanbul skyline.

items.title: ASX 200 down after Wall Street rallies to record highs, oil up

children.content: The ASX 200 has lost ground, despite both the S&P 500 and tech-centric Nasdaq indexes ending last week with new record highs, while oil prices are surging with Brent crude just below $US107 a barrel. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A stock trader looks down at a tablet as he works on the floor of a busy stock exchange while surrounded by screens.

items.title: Indigenous veteran booed at Anzac Day service 'mortified' by heckling

children.content: The Whadjuk Noongar elder who was booed while giving a Welcome to Country at Perth's Anzac Day dawn service says she had a right to speak at the event and will not back down.

image.altText: Di Ryder

items.title: Sabastian Sawe ran the marathon in an 'unbelievable time'. Here's why we can believe in it

children.content: Sabastian Sawe has smashed through one of athletics' unbreakable barriers by running a marathon faster than two hours. Some call it unbelievable, but the Kenyan runner has done everything he can to ensure it stands the test of time.

gallery.altText: Sabastian Sawe stands next to his record time
Sabastian Sawe holds out his hands
Sabastian Sawe runs past Big Ben

thumbnail.altText: Sabastian Sawe stands next to his record time

Sabastian Sawe runs past Big Ben

items.title: Frustration with war effort sparks renewed push to bring down Netanyahu — as it happened

children.content: Former Israeli prime ministers Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid are merging their two parties into a new entity called "Together" in a bid to defeat Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu at the next election.

image.altText: Two smiling Israeli men in suits wave to supporters as they stand in front of a blue and white background.

items.title: 'Membership to nothing': The multi-million-dollar Wild West of rewards clubs

children.content: "Rewards clubs" have exploded in Australia, claiming to have given away hundreds of millions in prizes, but they operate with almost no legal scrutiny.

image.altText: A large 4 wheel drive in a showroom clouded in smoke with dramatic red lighting.

items.title: An oil shock, a federal budget and the recession we may have to have

children.content: Accompanying the oil shock unfolding in the Strait of Hormuz is a profound change in the way fiscal and monetary policymakers operate. And as the government prepares the budget with the impact of the Iran war still unfolding, a recession may be ahead.

image.altText: A man in a suit from a side profile with his chin jutted out defiantly.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Magnitude-6.1 earthquake and raging wildfires strike Japan's north

children.content: A preliminary magnitude-6.1 earthquake struck northern Japan early today, the US Geological Survey has reported, but there are no immediate reports of fatalities or damage.

image.altText: A firefighter in a forest in Japan spraying water onto burnt areas

items.title: The games developer who shuns Australian stereotypes

children.content: Developer Gregory Louden always wanted to work in games, but it was a sidestep into filmmaking that helped him secure his dream job at Finnish studio Housemarque.

gallery.altText: A man in science fiction armour kneels down on the ground in a desert, a video game screenshot

items.title: 'I know it's illegal': Artist behind 'Aussie' posters reveals new target

children.content: Peter Drew has been hanging his controversial posters around Australia for 10 years, and has no plans to stop.

image.altText: A man with dark hair and a big smile

items.title: 'She's like Will Ferrell': Why Kitty Flanagan said yes to Anne Edmonds

children.content: Everything Anne Edmonds has done over the last two decades has been leading to Bad Company, an unfettered satire of the theatre world the comedian created, wrote and stars in alongside Kitty Flanagan.

image.altText: Kitty Flanagan, left, crosses her arms next to Anne Edmonds, right, as both stand in a theatre.

items.title: Maccas wins battle to set up in 'world's coolest street'

children.content: The fast food giant has been cleared to set up an outlet in the Melbourne suburb of Northcote after successfully challenging a council decision to block its restaurant plans.

gallery.altText: An architectural rendering of a McDonald's store on High Street in Northcote.
Street signs for Rathdowne Records, a woodfired pizza restaurant, an Indian restaurant and Victoria Bitter.

thumbnail.altText: An architectural rendering of a McDonald's store on High Street in Northcote.
Street signs for Rathdowne Records, a woodfired pizza restaurant, an Indian restaurant and Victoria Bitter.

items.title: Who's who in Tasmania's upper house elections this weekend

children.content: Voters in the Legislative Council divisions of Huon and Rosevears will head to the polls on Saturday. The outcome of the elections could determine the future of the greyhound industry in Tasmania.

image.altText: Six cards with candidate faces on them.

items.title: Marine sand proposal worries residents after lack of consultation

children.content: A Dutch dredging company acknowledges concerns from some community members in northern Western Australia that public meetings should have been held earlier.

image.altText: A man sits next to some reeds near a river

items.title: Law-breaking drivers to be targeted amid horror year on Queensland roads

children.content: The ABC last year revealed a sharp drop in road safety enforcement hours from police in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic.

image.altText: Police officers at checkpoint with driver line-up at Queensland-NSW border at Coolangatta.

items.title: PNG PM says Luai's visit 'sends a message' ahead of Chiefs' NRL entry

children.content: The Wests Tigers playmaker is the Chiefs' top target ahead of their NRL entry in 2028, and the Papua New Guinea prime minister says his trip to PNG shows the interest is mutual.

image.altText: Jarome Luai salutes with his right hand

items.title: Why the bedroom is an 'invisible workplace' for women

children.content: There are biological and psychological reasons behind why women experience more disrupted sleep than men, but experts say the mental load is another powerful yet less obvious contributor.

gallery.altText: illustration of woman lying awake next to her sleeping partner

thumbnail.altText: illustration of woman lying awake next to her sleeping partner

items.title: SA Police search waterways for knives linked to alleged murder of Caleb Leriano

children.content: SA Police will search waterways in Adelaide's south today to find three knives they believe are connected to the alleged murder of 24-year-old Caleb Leriano.

image.altText: A young man with glasse smiles at the camera in a bright room

items.title: Pogačar looks unbeatable, but Seixas could be France's next new hope

children.content: Tadej Pogačar claims a third monument win out of four for the year at Liège-Bastogne-Liège, but a new power is rising in the form of 19-year-old Paul Seixas, with an unlikely Tour de France tilt seemingly on the cards.

image.altText: Tadej Pogacar looks behind him at Paul Seixas

items.title: Australian surgeon returns for fourth Gaza mission despite PTSD

children.content: Despite suffering PTSD from his previous visits to Gaza, Herwig Drobetz is returning to Palestine to help a medical system at breaking point.

gallery.altText: 29 nurses and doctors in scrubs, male and female standing, facing the camera in front of tents on sandy soil

thumbnail.altText: 29 nurses and doctors in scrubs, male and female standing, facing the camera in front of tents on sandy soil

items.title: Trump turmoil is political kryptonite in Australia

children.content: As Donald Trump's approval in Australia sinks, Anthony Albanese needs to walk a careful line: keep voters convinced of the merits of the alliance, while avoiding the impression he is being too reverential.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese takes a selfie with a smiling Donald Trump

items.title: Mother's impassioned plea after young daughter's cerebral palsy diagnosis

children.content: Ayla doesn't let disability slow her down, but her mother wants her to have the same opportunities as others.

image.altText: A girl in a blue and white dress, with pink glasses smiling while sitting in a park

items.title: Farmers examine sheep for magic numbers to navigate drought

children.content: With a quick feel of a sheep's back, farmers score their flock, finding the magic numbers to help them navigate record-breaking drought, and now diesel price spikes.

image.altText: A man in a shed feels a sheep's back

items.title: Spanish derby descends into chaos after keeper punches opponent in face

children.content: The Aragonese derby between Real Zaragoza and Huesca descends into a wild brawl after Zaragoza goalkeeper Esteban Andrada punches Huesca skipper Jorge Pulido in the face.

image.altText: Esteban Andrada punches Jorge Pulido

items.title: Australia to sell fleet of Bushmaster armoured vehicles to the Dutch

children.content: Australian Bushmaster armoured vehicles are being sent to the Netherlands following a request to Australia by the Dutch government.

items.title: China's 'quirky' fix for property crisis rewards marathon runners

children.content: Local governments in China are offering marathon runners, AI experts and tennis players subsidies to purchase unwanted properties amid a lingering real estate downturn.

items.title: Mental impairment law falling short for man found not guilty but imprisoned

children.content: WA legal experts say the Criminal Law Mental Impairment Act isn't working as intended, citing a case in Broome where a man was found not criminally responsible but still jailed.

items.title: Promise of $1b to fix Victorian roads in upcoming budget

children.content: The funding package also includes nearly $37 million dedicated to cleaning up graffiti along major roads.
